input.background: The pets and stress illustrative example found a significant difference among the three groups (P<0.001). Now we conduct post hoc comparisons with the LSD method. Recall that group 1= pet present, group 2= friend present, and group 3= neither pet nor friend present.
input.question: The second post-hoc comparison is H0: μ1=μ3 against Ha: μ1≠μ3.

output.answer: In testing, H0: μ1=μ3, P=0.010. The 95% confidence interval for μ1−μ3 is (−15.8 to −2.3).

group.description: The categorical variable representing the three experimental groups.

μ1.value: Mean stress level for group 1 (pet present)
μ1.description: The mean stress level for the group where a pet is present.

μ3.value: Mean stress level for group 3 (neither pet nor friend present)
μ3.description: The mean stress level for the group where neither a pet nor a friend is present.

H0.description: The null hypothesis stating that the mean stress levels for group 1 and group 3 are equal.

Ha.description: The alternative hypothesis stating that the mean stress levels for group 1 and group 3 are not equal.

meta_info.caption: Post hoc comparisons, LSD method (Pets and stress).
